etymology.description: 主(主) (zhǔ, "master; main") connotes a person with authority or central stature, while 任(任) (rèn, "responsibility; appoint") indicates the act of holding a position of responsibility or being appointed to a role. Together, 主任(主任) (zhǔrèn, "director; person in charge") suggests someone who is the main person responsible for overseeing tasks or activities, thus acting as a director or person in charge.

examples.chinese: 学校的主任(學校的主任)负责管理所有的行政事务(負責管理所有的行政事務)。
examples.pinyin: Xuéxiào de zhǔrèn fùzé guǎnlǐ suǒyǒu de xíngzhèng shìwù.
examples.english: The school's director is responsible for overseeing all administrative affairs.

examples.chinese: 我们需要一个新的项目主任(我們需要一個新的項目主任)，以确保工作顺利进行(以確保工作順利進行)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào yī ge xīn de xiàngmù zhǔrèn, yǐ quèbǎo gōngzuò shùnlì jìnxíng.
examples.english: We need a new project director to ensure the work proceeds smoothly.

examples.chinese: 会议上，主任宣布了新的政策(會議上，主任宣佈了新的政策)。
examples.pinyin: Huìyì shàng, zhǔrèn xuānbùle xīn de zhèngcè.
examples.english: At the meeting, the director announced the new policy.
